commit.message:
fix(cmi): correct sample playback speed — was 11x too slow ("whale")

Root cause: CMI hardware pitch model reads wave_ram at ~1220 samples/sec
for middle C, designed for 128-sample page loops. Real Fairlight samples
at 14080 Hz would play at 0.087x speed — 13 seconds for a 1-second sample.

Fix: Added sample_mode flag — when a sample >256 bytes is loaded,
processSample() uses linear addressing with sample_inc = playRate/sampleRate
where playRate = 14080 * (freq/261.63). Hardware page-stepping mode preserved
for built-in waveforms.

Verified: 261 Hz output for MIDI 60 with 14080 Hz test sine (ratio 0.998x).

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Smooth pattern editor scrolling for GT Ultra playback

Added FormatPlaybackState singleton — a lightweight format-agnostic
playback state that any format engine can write to. The
PatternEditorCanvas RAF loop reads it every frame (no React props in

- FT2Toolbar: sets FormatPlaybackState playing on play/stop

fix: correct SurfaceNormal BVH face selection and add SN mismatch CI guard (#269)

* fix: correct SurfaceNormal BVH face selection and add SN mismatch CI guard

Phase 2 of the SurfaceNormal optimization (commit 7f1e867) used a pure
BVH triangle-to-face mapping (BVHFindNearestFaceBounds / fTriangleFaceIdx)
to identify the nearest face in O(log N_triangles).  This is fundamentally
incorrect near face boundaries: the tessellation error fBVHDeflection
(= 0.01 × max_face_bbox_diagonal) means the nearest tessellated triangle
may belong to a different analytical face than the true nearest face.
For g4tubs this produced 1275/2048 wrong normals; summed across all curved
fixtures the benchmark reported 13 937 SurfaceNormal mismatches.

Fix (Phase 2):
  Replace BVHFindNearestFaceBounds with BVH-seeded TryFindClosestFace:
  - BVHLowerBoundDistance(p) gives mesh lower bound bvhLB (~0 for a
    surface point).
  - seedDist = bvhLB + 2*fBVHDeflection is a valid upper bound on the
    true distance to the nearest surface point.
  - TryFindClosestFace(fFaceBoundsCache, p, seedDist) prunes distant
    faces via AABB before the BRepExtrema call, then uses exact
    BRepExtrema on all candidates — guaranteeing the correct face is
    identified regardless of tessellation approximation errors.

  Add maxDistance parameter to TryFindClosestFace (default kInfinity,
  fully backward-compatible) so callers can supply a BVH-derived upper
  bound as an initial pruning threshold.

  Remove BVHFindNearestFaceBounds, fTriangleFaceIdx (no longer needed;
  fTriangleSet is still used by BVHLowerBoundDistance).

CI guard:
  bench_navigator already returned EXIT_FAILURE when the ValidationReport
  contained SN mismatch errors, but only up to max_reported_mismatches (8)
  errors per fixture were added.  Add an explicit check on the summed
  surface_normal_mismatch_count across all fixtures so that any non-zero
  SN mismatch total causes EXIT_FAILURE, independent of the ValidationReport
  error-propagation path.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix: add explicit CI guards for all correctness mismatch counts in bench_navigator

The existing HasErrors(aggregate_report) check is the primary guard, but
each ValidationReport carries at most max_reported_mismatches (8) errors per
fixture, leaving the raw per-fixture counts as the only complete record.
Three specific gaps existed beyond the SurfaceNormal mismatch check added
in the previous commit:

- Exit-normal mismatches (normal_mismatch_count) share the combined
  mismatch_count cap with intersection/distance mismatches; if 8
  intersection errors exhaust the cap, exit-normal errors receive zero
  ValidationReport entries, making HasErrors() blind to them.
- Ray intersection/distance and Inside(p) mismatches both carry at least
  one ValidationReport error per fixture with any mismatch, but rely on
  the same uncertain error-propagation path that failed to catch the SN
  regression (commit 7f1e867).

Fix: after PrintNavigationReport, sum the raw mismatch counts from
state.summaries (skipping expected-failure fixtures whose errors are
legitimately reclassified to warnings) and return EXIT_FAILURE if any
non-zero count is found, printing one error line per affected method.
Also update the SurfaceNormal check from the previous commit to exclude
expected-failure fixtures consistently.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Fix GT Ultra silent playback: initialize editorInfo in gt_play()

gt_play() didn't initialize editorInfo.maxSIDChannels, adparam, or
multiplier before starting the sequencer. When .sng files lack the
ID=0x1f extended info block, these stay at 0 (the C global default).

With maxSIDChannels=0, the playroutine() channel loop in gplay.c
skips ALL channels — no notes are sequenced, producing a brief thud
(initial SID gate) then silence.

Added the same safety defaults already used by gt_export_prg/sid:
  maxSIDChannels=3, adparam=0x0f00, multiplier=1

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Persist matrix animation state across runs and align to terminal top (#343)

The matrix animation was reset on every `App::new()` and its coordinates
were viewport-relative, causing it to jump/restart between prompts
instead of appearing stationary against the terminal background.

## Changes

**State persistence**
- Removed `reset_matrix_anim_state()` call from `App::new()` — the
global `MATRIX_ANIM_STATE` now lives for the terminal session, not
per-prompt invocation
- Removed the now-dead `reset_matrix_anim_state` function

**Terminal-absolute coordinates**
- `apply_matrix_anim` now takes `viewport_top: u16` and
`terminal_height: u16`
- `state.update()` receives `terminal_height` so tendril `y`-positions
are in terminal-absolute rows (0 = terminal top)
- When writing tendrils into the content buffer, rows above
`viewport_top` are skipped via `.skip(viewport_top as usize)`; buffer
index = `term_row - viewport_top`

**Buffer sizing**
- Buffer is extended to `terminal_height - viewport_top` rows (fills
viewport-top → terminal-bottom), replacing the hardcoded `40`
- Early return when `viewport_top >= terminal_height` (no visible area)

**Call-site plumbing**
- `create_content` gains `viewport_top` and `terminal_height` params
- Run loop passes `frame_area.y` and `last_terminal_area.height`

inbound webhook route was computing HMAC signatures from an empty buffer,
causing all webhook signature verifications to fail.

commit.message:
feat: configuration hot reload (#179)

* Initial plan

* feat(core): add ReloadableRoutingTable trait and hot-reload support to DynamicRoutingTable

Add a new `ReloadableRoutingTable` trait in `bitrouter-core` that allows
replacing the inner routing table at runtime. `DynamicRoutingTable` now
stores its inner table behind `Arc<RwLock<_>>` so it can be atomically
swapped via `reload()` while preserving dynamic routes.

Includes two new tests: `reload_replaces_inner_table` and
`reload_preserves_dynamic_routes`.

Co-authored-by: takasaki404 <193405421+takasaki404@users.noreply.github.com>
Agent-Logs-Url: https://github.com/bitrouter/bitrouter/sessions/19527a78-4d7e-4360-8197-2ab9f9232272

* feat(cli): add `bitrouter reload` command for configuration hot-reload

Add the `Reload` CLI command that sends a reload signal to a running
daemon. On Unix this uses SIGHUP; on Windows a flag file is written.

The server spawns a background reload listener that waits for the signal,
re-reads the configuration file from disk, and atomically swaps the inner
routing table via `ReloadableRoutingTable::reload()`. Dynamic routes are
preserved across reloads.

Key changes:
- `DaemonManager::reload()` sends the platform reload signal
- `AppRuntime::serve_with_reload()` wires the reload callback
- `ServerPlan::with_reload()` accepts a type-erased reload closure
- `shutdown_signal` now coexists with a `reload_listener` task
- Updated README with the new `reload` command

Co-authored-by: takasaki404 <193405421+takasaki404@users.noreply.github.com>
Agent-Logs-Url: https://github.com/bitrouter/bitrouter/sessions/19527a78-4d7e-4360-8197-2ab9f9232272

* fix(server): remove explicit `ref` in implicitly-borrowing pattern

Use `&paths` instead of `ref p` to fix compilation on Windows where
the newer Rust edition disallows explicit `ref` binding modifiers
when the match is already implicitly borrowing.

Co-authored-by: takasaki404 <193405421+takasaki404@users.noreply.github.com>
Agent-Logs-Url: https://github.com/bitrouter/bitrouter/sessions/f092dc62-e4cb-4f8f-a0e1-67940e9b8c88

set WHOOP_WEBHOOK_SECRET separately — the existing WHOOP_CLIENT_SECRET

commit.message:
perf: accelerate SurfaceNormal(p) with BVH face identification (#265)

* perf: accelerate SurfaceNormal(p) with BVH face identification

Phase 1 – all-planar fast path:
  When fAllFacesPlanar is true, find the closest face by minimum
  gp_Pln::Distance and return the precomputed fb.outwardNormal.
  Eliminates all OCCT solver calls for planar solids (g4box, g4trap,
  g4trd, g4para) — expected improvement from ~100–165 ms to <1 ms.

Phase 2 – BVH face identification for curved solids:
  * Extend PointToMeshDistance to track the winning triangle index
    (myBestIndex) and expose it via BestIndex().
  * Build fTriangleFaceIdx in ComputeBounds() immediately after the
    BVH triangle set: for each face (in the same TopExp_Explorer order
    as fFaceBoundsCache) append the face-cache index once per triangle,
    giving an O(1) triangle→face lookup at query time.
  * Add BVHFindNearestFaceBounds(p): traverses the BVH in
    O(log N_triangles) and returns a pointer to the nearest FaceBounds.
  * SurfaceNormal(p) now calls BVHFindNearestFaceBounds then runs
    GeomAPI_ProjectPointOnSurf on the single identified face (instead
    of TryFindClosestFace which ran BRepExtrema on every candidate face)
    and uses TryGetOutwardNormal(fb.adaptor, ...) with the cached adaptor
    (fast overload, skipping BRepAdaptor_Surface construction).
  * Retains the original TryFindClosestFace path as a fallback when the
    BVH is unavailable.

Also documents both patterns in AGENTS.md.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* perf: apply review feedback to SurfaceNormal BVH optimisation (#266)

* Initial plan

* perf: reserve fTriangleFaceIdx capacity and extract projectAndGetNormal lambda

Address two review feedback items from PR #265:

1. Reserve fTriangleFaceIdx before insert loop in ComputeBounds() using
   fTriangleSet->Size() to avoid repeated reallocations for heavily
   tessellated shapes.

2. Extract the shared "project point → get (u,v) → evaluate outward
   normal" sequence into a projectAndGetNormal lambda in SurfaceNormal(p),
   eliminating code duplication between the BVH path and fallback path.
   The lambda takes an optional BRepAdaptor_Surface* so the BVH path uses
   the pre-built cached adaptor (fast overload) while the fallback constructs
   one on the fly.

Co-authored-by: wdconinc <4656391+wdconinc@users.noreply.github.com>
